landing.headline: A focused workspace for converting text into Braille and saving every result.
landing.subheadline: Sign in with Supabase Auth, upload text, documents, or photos, generate Braille output for text and math, and keep a searchable personal archive with stats, exports, and keyboard shortcuts inside the dashboard.

smart.title: Smart conversion workspace
smart.description: Paste text directly or upload .txt, .pdf, and image files with readability suggestions before saving.

search.title: Search, tags, and bulk actions
search.description: Filter by archive, favorite, date, source, and mode, then tag, export, or archive documents in bulk.

dashboard.title: Personalized dashboard
dashboard.description: Choose dark or light mode, theme accent, default view, and card density for your own Braille workspace.

dashboard.continue: Continue from your latest Braille activity or start a new conversion.
dashboard.activeConversions: Conversions currently kept in the main library.
dashboard.emptyDescription: Upload text, PDF, or images to create your first Braille conversion. Your results will be listed here as recent activity.
dashboard.graphsHelper: Read graphs with normal and Braille narration
dashboard.savedConversionsLoadFailed: Your saved conversions could not be loaded right now.
dashboard.workspaceQuotaMessage: You are using {count}/{limit} saved document slots in the dashboard library.
dashboard.firstUseGuideDismissed: The first-use guide has been dismissed.
dashboard.onboardingGuideShownAgain: The onboarding guide will be shown again when the library is empty.
dashboard.onboardingPreferenceUpdateFailed: The onboarding preference could not be updated.

upload.workspaceTitle: Convert text, documents, and visuals with less friction
upload.workspaceDescription: The convert action now stays visible at the top for short inputs, and longer materials can open into a page-by-page comparison reader after processing.
upload.sourcePlaceholder: Paste or type the text you want to convert.
upload.dropzoneHint: Drag and drop a file here
upload.dropzoneSubhint: Supports .txt, .pdf and images. You can also open camera capture.
upload.unsupportedDocumentFormat: Please choose a valid file: .pdf, .txt, .jpg, .jpeg, or .png.
upload.unsupportedCameraFormat: Camera capture accepts image files only.
upload.cameraPermissionDenied: Camera access is blocked. Please allow camera permission and try again.
upload.previewPlaceholder: Converted Braille appears here instantly after processing.
upload.supportedFiles: Please upload a .txt, .pdf, or image file.
upload.addTextOrChooseFile: Add text or choose a file before converting.
upload.brailleCopiedMessage: The converted Braille output was copied to the clipboard.
upload.clipboardBlocked: Clipboard access is blocked in this browser tab.
upload.exportFailed: The export could not be completed.
upload.conversionSavedMessage: {name} is ready to review, copy, or export.
upload.conversionFailed: The upload could not be converted right now.

documents.searchPlaceholder: Search by title, source text, Braille output, or tags
documents.createFirst: Create your first Braille library entry
documents.deleteDescription: This permanently removes the saved source text, tags, and Braille output from your workspace history.
documents.supabaseMissing: Supabase is not connected yet. Refresh the page and try again.
documents.updateFailed: The document update could not be completed.
documents.clipboardBlocked: Clipboard access is blocked in this browser tab.
documents.restoredToActive: Document restored to the active library.
documents.selectedMovedToArchive: Selected documents moved to the archive.
documents.selectedRestoredFromArchive: Selected documents restored from the archive.
documents.exportFailed: The export could not be completed.
documents.emptyLibraryDescription: Your library is empty. Start your first conversion to fill it up!
documents.comparisonNoteAligned: Original text and Braille are grouped into matching rows so the same content stays on the same line.
documents.comparisonNoteSpacing: Extra spacing in the original text is normalized for alignment, so comparison stays cleaner.
documents.comparisonNoteHover: Hover or focus a word block to follow the matching Braille block more easily.

review.title: Comparison reader
review.description: Review long text and multi-page documents page by page with the original alphabet and Braille output visible at the same time.
review.freshResultMessage: The conversion finished and opened in this dedicated reading view.
review.noDocumentSelected: No document was selected for review.
review.loadFailed: The document review view could not be opened right now.
review.downloadFailed: The document could not be downloaded.
review.readerNotNeeded: This document does not need the dedicated reader.
review.readerNotNeededDescription: Short content is usually easier to inspect in the standard preview. You can still return to the conversion or library screens.
review.noteAlignedRows: Each page is split into matching reading rows so the original text and Braille stay aligned line by line.
review.noteSpacing: The original page uses normalized spacing to keep the comparison easier to scan.
review.noteSync: When you move between pages, both panels advance together and keep the same section open.
review.jumpInvalid: Enter digits only for page navigation.
review.footerHint: When you change pages, both panels move together so the source content and translation stay in sync.
review.copyOriginalSuccess: The normal text on this page was copied to the clipboard.
review.copyBrailleSuccess: The Braille text on this page was copied to the clipboard.
review.downloadOriginalSuccess: The normal text on this page was downloaded.
review.downloadBrailleSuccess: The Braille text on this page was downloaded.
review.downloadBrailleBrfSuccess: The Braille output for this document was downloaded as .brf.
review.copyBlocked: Clipboard access is blocked in this tab.

graphs.heroTitle: Upload a graph and get a mathematical reading
graphs.heroDescription: A user can upload nearly any graph, function sketch, or statistical chart image. The system reads it, produces a teacher-friendly normal alphabet explanation, and then converts that same narration into Braille.
graphs.workflowStepTwo: 2. Analyze axes, trends, and critical points.
graphs.workflowStepThree: 3. Compare the normal alphabet and Braille narration side by side.
graphs.dropzoneDescription: It can be a line graph, bar chart, coordinate-plane graphic, function curve, or another chart type. A clear screenshot or photo is enough.
graphs.invalidFile: Please choose an image file for the graph analysis.
graphs.selectGraphFirst: Select a graph image before starting the analysis.
graphs.analysisFailed: The graph could not be interpreted right now.
graphs.copyBlocked: Clipboard access is blocked in this tab.
graphs.previewPlaceholder: Your selected graph will appear here. The panel also highlights how the narration is meant to support quick classroom verification.
graphs.tipOneDescription: The system extracts graph type, axis meaning, trend changes, intercepts, peaks and valleys, and visible comparisons across the graph.
graphs.tipTwoDescription: Check the normal alphabet explanation against the image, then compare the matching Braille narration beside it to confirm the conversion stayed faithful.
graphs.loginToSave: Sign in to save this graph reading into your library.
graphs.savedMessage: The Graph Reader result was saved to your library.
graphs.saveFailed: The graph reading could not be saved right now.
graphs.offlineFallbackTitle: Basic no-API mode is available too
graphs.offlineFallbackDescription: If advanced AI analysis is unavailable, the app falls back to browser OCR to read the title, axis text, and visible numbers, then prepares a basic interpretation. This mode is more limited, but it does not return empty-handed.
graphs.emptyResultTitle: Upload a graph to start reading it
graphs.emptyResultDescription: Graph Reader is built for simple textbook-style coordinate graphs first. Upload an image, analyze it, then inspect the linked Latin and Braille explanation below.
graphs.syncHelper: Hover, focus, or tap a segment in either column to highlight its matching pair.

settings.displayNamePlaceholder: How should the workspace greet you?
settings.preferencesSavedMessage: Display settings and default workspace behavior were updated.
settings.preferencesFailedMessage: The profile settings could not be saved.
settings.profileDescription: Manage your profile information and personal details
settings.appearanceDescription: Customize how the interface looks and feels
settings.notificationsDescription: Control how and when you receive notifications

auth.loginSubtitle: Access saved conversions, upload new documents, and manage your Braille output.
auth.registerSubtitle: Sign up with email and password so each conversion stays linked to your own workspace.
auth.displayNamePlaceholder: How should your workspace greet you?
auth.redirectingToLogin: Sending you to the login page.
auth.enterEmailFirst: Enter your email first, then resend the confirmation message.
auth.couldNotResend: We could not resend the confirmation email.
auth.confirmationResent: Confirmation email sent again. Check your inbox and spam folder.
auth.accountCreated: Account created. You can now log in with the same email and password.
auth.confirmEmail: Account created. Please confirm your email before logging in.
auth.sessionDelay: Session check is taking longer than expected. You can continue signing in while the connection finishes in the background.

wordAddin.title: Braille Vision for Word
wordAddin.subtitle: Convert selected text and math inside Microsoft Word.
wordAddin.description: The add-in keeps the existing Word workflow, but with the new lilac workspace. Select content in Word, trigger a conversion, then insert or export the result.
wordAddin.workspaceDesignedForWord: This workspace is designed to run inside Microsoft Word.
wordAddin.openInWord: Open this page inside Word before using the add-in actions.
wordAddin.documentEmpty: The current Word document appears to be empty.
wordAddin.noMathExpression: No mathematical expression was returned from the backend.
wordAddin.conversionReadySessionLoading: Conversion is ready. Session sync is still loading.
wordAddin.conversionReadyLoginToSave: Conversion is ready. Log in to save it to the dashboard history.
wordAddin.conversionReadySynced: Conversion is ready and synced to your dashboard history.
wordAddin.resultInserted: Result inserted into the current Word document.
wordAddin.selectedTextDesc: Reads the active Word selection and returns Braille output.
wordAddin.mathSelectionDesc: Use the existing math backend for selected equations or expressions.
wordAddin.fullDocumentDesc: Reads the full Word document and generates a text-to-Braille result.
wordAddin.keepBackendOpen: Keep the Python backend and this Word gateway open while testing. Sideloading still uses the same manifest flow.
wordAddin.wordContentSending: Word content is being sent through the existing Braille backend.
wordAddin.noConversionDescription: Open the task pane inside Word, select text or math, and run one of the actions on the left. The results will appear here with insert, copy, listen, and download controls.
